title: Anthemis artemisioides
intro: Anthemis artemisioides, commonly known as Chamois Chamomile or False Chamomile, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, characterized by its compact, shrub-like growth habit and delicate, daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and calming properties, providing relief from anxiety, insomnia, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Anthemis artemisioides include flavonoids, terpenoids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as topical creams and ointments for soothing skin irritations and wounds.
taxonomy: Anthemis artemisioides belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This family consists of over 23,000 species, making it one of the largest families of flowering plants. The genus Anthemis is a group of flowering plants native to the Mediterranean region and other parts of the world. Anthemis artemisioides is a specific species within this genus, characterized by its unique features and growth habits. Classification of Anthemis artemisioides is further refined at the species level, considering its morphology, distribution, and genetic characteristics.
uses: Anthemis artemisioides, commonly known as wormwood chamomile or chamomile wormwood, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Its essential oils and other compounds are believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and calming properties. The plant is used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, anxiety, and insomnia. In addition, Anthemis artemisioides is sometimes employed as a natural remedy for skin irritations and wounds. More research is needed to confirm the efficacy and potential side effects of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Anthemis artemisioides, commonly known as Chamomile Daisy, has been reported to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its extracts have been studied for their ability to soothe digestive issues and promote relaxation. Some traditional uses of the plant include treating minor skin irritations and reducing anxiety. properties: Anthemis artemisioides is known for its potential therapeutic properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is reported to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's extracts have been studied for their antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. Traditional remedies often use Anthemis artemisioides to alleviate digestive issues and soothe skin irritations. Further research is needed to confirm the full extent of its therapeutic benefits.
constituents: Anthemis artemisioides, also known as the chamomile daisy, contains various active constituents, primarily essential oils and flavonoids. The essential oils in Anthemis artemisioides include chamazulene, which is known for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Additionally, the plant contains apigenin and luteolin, two flavonoids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These active constituents contribute to the plant's potential therapeutic uses, including soothing digestive issues and reducing inflammation. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of Anthemis artemisioides' active constituents.

preparations: Anthemis artemisioides is a plant species used in herbal preparations, primarily for its medicinal and culinary properties.
